explanation: Sarah Wilson is the strongest candidate for this role. She covers all 5 required skills with 4 years of relevant experience and holds the AWS Certified Developer certification. Her project portfolio — including a fully deployed microservices platform and data pipeline — directly demonstrates the kind of work this role demands. Strongly recommend for interview.

David Kim brings 6 years of strong systems experience and exceptional credential verification. While FastAPI and PostgreSQL are gaps, his Python proficiency and mature project portfolio suggest he can close them quickly. His verification and growth scores are the highest in the pool. Worth interviewing — especially if the team can mentor on the web framework side.

Raj Patel matches on Python, FastAPI, Docker and AWS — and his deployed ML API projects show solid backend capability. The only hard gap is PostgreSQL, which is learnable. His ML specialty is a bonus if the backend role touches data pipelines. Consider for interview, especially if the team values ML-aware engineers.

Emma is a highly verified cloud specialist with 5 years of experience, but the skill gap to this backend role is significant — Python, FastAPI, and PostgreSQL are all missing. Her alternative role fit for Cloud Engineer or DevOps is near-perfect. Recommend redirecting her to JOB004 rather than stretching her into this role.

Meera is an excellent data engineer with strong Python and AWS, but her background skews heavily towards data pipelines rather than backend API development. Missing FastAPI and Docker are meaningful gaps for this role. Her alternative role fit for Data Engineer (JOB003) is a 99% match — redirect her there.

John has the right stack (Python, FastAPI, Docker, PostgreSQL) but is 1 year short on experience and lacks AWS — the most critical preferred skill. His projects are functional but lack deployment evidence, which keeps his verification score low. Consider as a reserve candidate or for a junior-track version of this role.

Alex is a strong data engineer whose skills (Spark, Airflow, Kafka) do not align with this backend role. FastAPI, Docker, and AWS are all missing. His growth score is high, indicating he learns quickly, but the skill gap is too wide for this position. Strongly recommend for JOB003 instead.

Priya is an early-career candidate with only 1 year of experience and a limited project portfolio. She is missing 3 of 5 required skills and 2 years of required experience. Not recommended for this mid-level role at this time. Consider revisiting in 12–18 months or for a junior opening.
